label: Firestore
description: Read and write documents in a Google Cloud Firestore database using a service account.

form.title: Connect Firestore
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Firestore collections via a GCP service account.

methods.label: Service Account JSON
methods.description: Paste a GCP service account key (JSON) with Firestore access. The simplest and most reliable path — no OAuth flow required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Service Account JSON

This is the recommended path. You create a service account in Google Cloud, grant it Firestore access, download a JSON key, and paste it below.

## Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Create service account**.
3. Give it a name like `anton-firestore` and click **Create and continue**.
4. On the **Grant access** step, assign one of these roles:
   - `Cloud Datastore User` — read/write access to Firestore in Datastore mode
   - `Firebase Admin SDK Administrator Service Agent` — broader Firebase/Firestore access
   - Or simply `Cloud Firestore Editor` / `Cloud Firestore Viewer` depending on your needs
5. Click **Done**.
6. Open the service account you just created, go to the **Keys** tab.
7. Click **Add key → Create new key → JSON**, then click **Create**.
8. A `.json` file downloads automatically — open it and copy its entire contents.
9. Paste the JSON into the **Service Account JSON** field below.
10. Enter your **GCP Project ID** (visible in the Cloud Console top nav, e.g. `my-project-123`).

## Notes

- The JSON key is the security boundary. Treat it like a password and rotate it in Cloud Console if you suspect exposure.
- If Firestore is in **Native mode** vs **Datastore mode**, the same service account works for both — just ensure the correct role is assigned.
- The key is shown only once at download time. If you lose it, generate a new one from the Keys tab.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service Account JSON
fields.description: Paste the full contents of the JSON key file downloaded from GCP IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Keys.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: GCP Project ID
fields.description: Your Google Cloud project ID — visible in the Cloud Console header or on the project dashboard.

fields.name: database_id
fields.label: Database ID
fields.description: The Firestore database ID. Leave as (default) unless you created a named database.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Google Sign-In)
methods.description: Authorize via your Google account in the browser. Requires setting up a Google Cloud OAuth client — more steps than the service account path.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OAuth 2.0

Use this method if you prefer authorizing with your Google account rather than a service account key. You must supply your own Google Cloud OAuth client credentials.

## Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and select your project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library** and enable the **Cloud Firestore API**.
3. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen** and configure an internal or external app. Add your email as a test user if external.
4. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
5. Choose **Desktop app** as the application type and give it a name.
6. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** shown.
7. Paste them into the fields below, along with your **GCP Project ID**.
8. Click **Submit**. A browser window opens for Google's consent screen — approve the requested scopes.

## Notes

- Scopes requested: `datastore` (Firestore read/write) and `cloud-platform` (broad GCP access).
- Refresh tokens are issued automatically so Anton can reconnect without re-authorizing.
- To revoke, visit [Google Account Permissions](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and remove Anton.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID — copied from the same row in Cloud Console Credentials.

fields.name: project_id
fields.label: GCP Project ID
fields.description: Your Google Cloud project ID — visible in the Cloud Console header or project dashboard.

fields.name: database_id
fields.label: Database ID
fields.description: The Firestore database ID. Leave as (default) unless you created a named database.
